PeerToPeerItem.description: The public file sharing software parameters to be verified.

version.title: Version
version.description: Version of the public file sharing software.

state.title: State
state.description:
State of the public file sharing software. The valid values are:
* **unspecified** - Does not verify the state of the software.
* **running** - Verifies if the public file sharing software is running on the client.
* **stopped** - Verifies if the public file sharing software is not running on the client.

continuousCheck.title: Continuously Check Result
continuousCheck.description: Determines whether to continuously check the result and end the session if it changes. When set to true, if the client does not respond for five minutes, the server ends the session.

softwareCheckInList.title: Check For Software In The List
softwareCheckInList.description:
Determines how the software check uses the list to send traffic. The valid values are:
* **pass-on-listed** - The traffic is sent to the successful branch if at least one software item in the list matches the software on the client system.
* **fail-on-unlisted** - In this case, the list functions as an allowlist. The traffic is sent to the fallback branch when any software not included in the list is found on the system.
* **fail-on-listed** - In this case, the list functions as a denylist. The traffic is sent to the fallback branch when any software item in the list is found on the client system.

configurations.title: Public File Sharing Parameters List
configurations.description: The public file sharing software parameters list to check on client endpoints.

expression.description:
The Tcl expression to choose a branch. The Tcl expression can contain one or more expressions (expr) or return commands.

An **expr** evaluates an expression and returns the result. A **return** simply returns the result and can be used to set the variable to a numeric value or string. When evaluating a branch rule, Access obtains session variables from system memory using the Tcl command **mcget**.

For example, expr {[mcget {session.client.js}] == 1 && [mcget {session.client.activex}] == 1 || [mcget {session.client.js}] == 1 && [mcget {session.client.plugin}] == 1}.

filename.title: File Name
filename.description: The file name to check. For example, C:\Windows\Demo\demofile.txt.

md5.title: MD5
md5.description: The MD5 checksum. It is a 32-digit hexadecimal value that provides easily computable verification of the identity of a file using a cryptographic hash algorithm. For example, the checksum for a zero-byte file is always d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e.

size.title: Size
size.description:
The size of the file in bytes. When set to 0, the item checks only for the file's existence. Otherwise, it checks the file size.

To check for a zero-byte file, specify the MD5 checksum for a zero-byte file in the md5 property. The MD5 checksum for a zero-byte file is always d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e.

dateModified.title: Date Modified
dateModified.description: The file's last modified date. When you use the default date of 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z, it is the same as specifying no date. The date must be translated to Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) and a 24-hour clock.

signer.title: Signer
signer.description: The signer for the file. No signer check is performed when this is not specified.

version.title: Version
version.description: The version of the file. For example, 9.0.8112.2904. This property can be left blank to omit checking for a version. Use this value along with the operation property to compare the checked file version with the specified version.

operation.title: Operation
operation.description:
The version comparison operation. The valid values are:

* **equal** - Check if the file version is the exact version number you specified.
* **lesser** - Check if the file version is greater than the version number you specified.
* **greater** - Check if the file version is less than the version number you specified.

continuousCheck.title: Continuous Check
continuousCheck.description: Determines whether to continuously check the result and end the session if it changes. When set to true, the file check recurs on the Windows system every 90 seconds. The server ends the session if the client does not respond for five minutes.

continuousCheck.description: Determines whether to continuously check the result and end the session if it changes. When set to true, the file check recurs on the Linux system every 5 seconds. The server ends the session if the client does not respond for five minutes.

uuid.description: A Universally Unique IDentifier in the string representation defined in RFC 4122.  The canonical representation uses lowercase characters.

caFile.title: CA File
caFile.description: Specifies the trusted certificate authorities that this certificate authority uses.

crlFile.title: CRL File
crlFile.description: Specifies the name of a file containing a list of revoked client certificates.

authenticateDepth.title: Certificate Authentication Depth
authenticateDepth.description: Specifies how many certificates the certificate authority allows to be chained.

updateCrl.title: Update CRL File for the CA
updateCrl.description: Specfies whether the certificate authority to automatically update the associated CRL file.

expression.description:
The Tcl expression to choose a branch. The Tcl expression can contain one or more expressions (expr) or return commands.

An **expr** evaluates an expression and returns the result. A **return** simply returns the result and can be used to set the variable to a numeric value or string. When evaluating a branch rule, Access obtains session variables from system memory using the Tcl command **mcget**.

For example, expr {[mcget {session.crldp.last.result}] == 1}.

allowElevation.title: Allow User Account Control elevation prompts
allowElevation.description: A UAC prompt for users with admin-level privileges is allowed.

caProfile.title: CA Profile
caProfile.description: The certificate authority profile for the particular machine certificate.

issuer.title: Match Issuer
issuer.description: The regular expression that is used to match the Issuer content against the specified pattern.

saveCertificate.title: Save Certificate
saveCertificate.description: The entire encrypted text of the machine certificate be saved in a session variable.

serialNumber.title: Match Serial Number
serialNumber.description: The serial number that must be an exact match for the certificate serial. The hex string must be specified in the same order as it is displayed by OpenSSL and Windows certificate tools.

storeLocation.title: Certificate Store Location
storeLocation.description: The type and location of the store that contains the certificate, either the local machine or the current user.

storeName.title: Certificate Store Name
storeName.description: The certificate store name that the action attempts to match. The certificate store can be a system store with a predefined name, such as MY, or a user-defined name. The store name can contain alphanumeric characters. The Machine Cert Auth action treats MY as the default store name for both Mac and Windows clients.

subjectAltName.title: Match subject Alt Name with FQDN
subjectAltName.description: The regular expression used to extract content from the first subgroup matched in the Subject Alternative Name, and then to compare the extracted content with the machine's FQDN.

subjectMatchFqdn.title: Match subject CN with FQDN
subjectMatchFqdn.description: The common name in the machine certificate matches the computer's fully qualified domain name (FQDN).

aclEntryProtocolType.description: The protocol to which the access control entry applies.

aclEntryAction.description:
The action to take when a match for this access control entry is encountered. The valid values are:

* **Allow** - Permit the traffic.
* **Continue** - Skip checking against this ACL's remaining access control entries and continue evaluation at the next ACL.
* **Discard** - Drop the packet silently.
* **Reject** - Drop the packet and send a TCP RST message on TCP flows or ICMP messages on UDP flows. Silently drop the packet on other protocols.
* **unspec** - Take no action.

aclEntryLog.description:
The log level that is logged when actions of this type occur. Valid values are:

* **config** - Logs the configuration of a matched entry.
* **none** - Logs nothing.
* **packet** - Logs the matched packet.
* **summary** - Logs the name and entry number of a matched ACL and ACL entry.
* **verbose** - Logs everything.

scheme.title: Scheme
scheme.description: The URI scheme for the access control entry.

hostName.description:
The hostname to which the access control entry applies. You can use the asterisk wildcard (*) to match zero or more characters and the question mark wildcard (?) to match a single character.

For example, *.siterequest.com matches siterequest.com with any prefix, such as www.siterequest.com, mail.siterequest.com, finance.siterequest.com, and others with the same pattern.

n?t.siterequest.com matches the hosts net.siterequest.com and not.siterequest.com, but not neet.siterequest.com, nt.siterequrest.com, or note.siterequest.com.
hostName.title: Host Name

paths.description:
The path or paths to which the access control entry applies. You can separate multiple paths with spaces, for example, /news /finance. You can also use the wildcard characters * and question mark (?) to represent multiple or single characters, respectively.

Example,
For a specific URI - /finance/content/earnings.asp
For a specific extension - *.jsp

* **unspec** - Take no action.

l4ServerSide.description: A soft reference, by name, to an L4 server-side object.

ldapSchemaAttrs.description: Access Policy Manager provides Active Directory-specific default values for these LDAP schema-specific attribute names. You can change them to reflect your schema.

groupMember.description: If the group object maintains a list of users that belong to it, specifies the value of the attribute that indicates this. Defaults to member.

groupMemberValue.description: If the    Group Member attribute is specified, this field specifies the attribute that is used to add users to a group. Defaults to dn.

groupMemberof.description: If the group object maintains membership in other groups, specifies the value of the membership attribute. Defaults to memberOf.

groupObjectClass.description: Specifies the value of the objectClass attribute for a group object. Defaults to group.

userMemberof.description: If the user object maintains a group membership, specifies the value of the membership attribute. Defaults to memberOf.

userObjectClass.description: Specifies the value of the objectClass attribute for a user object. Defaults to user.

name.description: Specifies the name of the AAA server. This option is required.

port.description: Specifies the port number of the AAA LDAP server. The default is 389.

tls.description: Specifies the TLS for server side. LDAPS is achieved by directing LDAP traffic over a virtual server that uses a server side TLS to communicate with the LDAP server.

timeout.description: Specifies a timeout interval (in seconds) for the AAA server after which the server closes a connection.  The default is 15.

load-balancing-mode.description:
The load balancing method.
 * `round-robin` - Round Robin method passes each new connection request to the next endpoint in line, eventually distributing connections evenly across the array of endpoints being load balanced.
 * `least-connections` - Least Connections method passes a new connection to the endpoint having the least number of active connections.
 * `predictive` - Predictive method uses the ranking method used by the Observed method, where endpoints are rated according to the number of current connections. However, with the Predictive method, the system analyzes the trend of the ranking over time, determining whether an endpoint's performance is currently improving or declining. The endpoints with performance rankings that are currently improving, rather than declining, receive a higher proportion of the connections.
 * `weighted-round-robin` - Weighted Round Robin method distributes connections over endpoints in a static rotation according to ratio weights.
 * `ratio-least-connections` - Ratio Least Connections method causes BIG-IP Next Central Manager to select the endpoint according to the ratio of the number of connections that each endpoint has active.
 * `ratio-session` - Ratio Session method selects the endpoint according to a ratio of the number of entries in the persistence table.
 * `fastest` - Fastest method selects an endpoint based on the least number of current sessions.

acctLockoutDuration.title: Account Lockout Duration
acctLockoutDuration.description: The duration in minutes the user is locked out after they hit the acctLockoutThreshold value. Once this time is elapsed the user is automatically unlocked. A value of 0 requires the account to be manually unlocked by an administrator.

acctLockoutThreshold.title: Account Lockout Threshold
acctLockoutThreshold.description: The number of failed logon attempts before the account is locked out. A locked-out account cannot be used until an administrator resets it or until the acctLockoutWindow for the account has expired. If this value is greater than 0, the acctLockoutDuration must be greater than or equal to the acctLockoutWindow.

acctLockoutWindow.title: Account Lockout Window
acctLockoutWindow.description: The number of minutes that must elapse after an account is locked due to unsuccessful logon attempts before resetting the acctLockoutThreshold counter. If the acctLockoutThreshold is greater than 0, the acctLockoutWindow must be less than or equal to the acctLockoutDuration.

adminName.title: Admin Name
adminName.description: The administrator name that has Active Directory administrative permissions. The administrator name is case-sensitive.

adminPassword.title: Admin Password
adminPassword.description: The administrator password associated with the domain name.

groupCacheTtl.title: Group Cache Lifetime
groupCacheTtl.description: The lifetime of a group cache, in days. The default lifetime is 30 days. This means that BIG-IP Next refreshes the Active Directory group cache every 30 days.

kdcValidation.title: KDC Validation
kdcValidation.description:
Determines whether to enable Kerberos KDC Validation. The Kerberos Key Distribution Center (KDC) is a network service that supplies session tickets and temporary session keys to users and computers within an Active Directory domain. The KDC runs on each domain controller and is responsible for authenticating users. The KDC validation allows you to prevent a KDC spoofing attack.

You configure a KDC validation by importing a keytab file that you exported from the Kerberos KDC. When you enable the KDC validation, after obtaining the ticket-granting ticket (TGT) and validating the user, BIG-IP Next requests a service ticket on behalf of the user. It validates the returned service ticket against the secret key for the KDC, which is stored in a keytab file. When the validation with the keytab file fails, the KDC server is considered untrusted, and the user is not authenticated.

keytabFileName.title: Keytab File
keytabFileName.description: A keytab file containing Kerberos encrypted keys. These are derived from the Kerberos password. It contains the service keys that the server uses to authenticate the client.

keytabFileContent.description: Keytab file content that contains Kerberos encrypted keys; (these are derived from the Kerberos password).

name.title: Server Name
name.description: The AD server name.

padataEncType.title: Kerberos Preauthentication Encryption Type
padataEncType.description: When this value is specified, BIG-IP Next includes Kerberos preauthentication data within the first authentication service request (AS-REQ) packet.

psoCacheTtl.title: Password Security Object Cache Lifetime
psoCacheTtl.description: The lifetime of a Password Security Object (PSO) cache.

serviceName.title: Service Name
serviceName.description: The Kerberos service name. This is required if kdcValidation is set to true.

poolName.title: Pool Name
poolName.description: A soft reference, by name, to a pool object.

monitors.title: Monitors

l4ServerSide.title: L4 Server Side
l4ServerSide.description: A soft reference, by name, to an L4 server-side object.

ignorePersistedWeight.title: Ignore Persisted Weight
ignorePersistedWeight.description: Determines whether to count the weight of persisted connections on pool members when making load balancing decisions.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: The number of seconds allowed to reach the Active Directory server initially.

source.title: CRLDP Server
source.description: The CRLDP server source. For a CRLDP Server Endpoint mode, the value is endpoint.

baseDn.title: Base DN
baseDn.description: A CRLDP base distinguished name for certificates that specify the CRL distribution point in the directory name (dirName) format. Access uses Base DN when the value of the X509v3 attribute crlDistributionPoints is of type dirName and tries to match the value of the crlDistributionPoints attribute to the Base DN value. An example of a Base DN value is cn=lxxx,dc=f5,dc=com.

reverseDn.title: Reverse DN
reverseDn.description: Determines the order in which the system should attempt to match the Base DN value to the value of the X509v3 attribute crlDistributionPoints. When set to true, Access matches the base DN from left to right or from the beginning of the DN string to accommodate dirName strings in certificates such as c=us,st=wa,l=sea,ou=f5,cn=xxx.

poolName.title: Pool Name
poolName.description: A soft reference, by name, to a pool object.

source.title: CRLDP Server Source
source.description: The CRLDP server source. For a CRLDP Server Certificate mode, the value is certificate.

dnsResolverName.title: DNS Resolver Name
dnsResolverName.description: The DNS resolver to be used by the server to resolve DNS names for endpoint URIs.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the AAA CRLDP server.

cacheExpire.title: Cache Timeout
cacheExpire.description: The number of seconds a CRL is cached.

useIssuer.title: Use Issuer
useIssuer.description: Determines whether the system extracts the CRL distribution point from the certificate of the client certificate issuer. When set to true, BIG-IP Next uses the CRLDP information provided by the issuer certificate instead of the client certificate.

allowNullcrl.title: Allow Null CRL
allowNullcrl.description: Determines whether a null CRL from the CRLDP server is considered a successful authentication.

verifySig.title: Verify Signature
verifySig.description: Determines whether the signature on the received CRL is verified. This parameter value must be true when using LDAP or HTTP to fetch CRL.

connTimeout.title: Connection Timeout
connTimeout.description: The number of seconds of inactivity the system allows before the connection times out. This parameter does not apply if you specify the source parameter value as endpoint in the serverConnection object.

updateInterval.title: Update Interval
updateInterval.description: The validity (in seconds) of the CRL file. To force the retrieval of a CRL file before the current CRL becomes obsolete, set this value to less than the CRL expiration time. If the value is zero (default), the CRLDP action uses the expiration time specified by the CA's CRL publishing parameters.

serverConnection.description: Determines how BIG-IP Next connects to the AAA CRLDP server. You can specify an endpoint to create pool members for High Availability or use a fully qualified HTTP or LDAP URL from the user/issuer certificate for the CRL location.

type.title: Key Type
type.description: Specifies the strength type of the key associated with the certificate.

bitLength.title: Bit length.
bitLength.description: Specifies the bit length of the key associated with the certificate.

curve.title: Key Certificate Curve
curve.description: Specifies the key certificate curve of the encryption associated with the certificate.

type.title: Key Strength Type
type.description: Specifies the type of key strength. The default value is curve.

curveName.title: Curve Name
curveName.description: Specifies the curve name representing the encryption associated with the certificate.

keyCertificatePEMContents.title: PEM Contents

key.title: Key PEM Content

certificate.title: X.509 PEM Content
certificate.description: Specifies the X.509 PEM content in a string of no more than 64,000 characters.

keyCertificateFileReferences.title: File References
keyCertificateFileReferences.description: Reports the file reference associated with the key and certificate.

keyFile.title: Specifies the key file in a string of no more than 64,000 characters.

certificateFile.description: Specifies the certificate file in a string of no more than 64,000 characters.

cipherstring.title: Cipher String
cipherstring.description: Specifies the cipher string for server-side SSL connections in a string of no more than 64,000 characters.

keyCertificatePairs.title: Certificate Key Pair
keyCertificatePairs.description: Specifies the certificate and key pair.

tlsVersions.title: TLS Version
tlsVersions.description: Specifies the TLS version information for these server-side TLS settings.

trustedCa.title: Trusted CA Bundle File
trustedCa.description: Specifies the trusted CA bundle file for authenticating TLS servers.

crlFile.title: CRL File
crlFile.description: Specifies the CRL file for authenticating TLS server certificates.

crlFileAllowExpired.title: Allow CRL File Expired
crlFileAllowExpired.description: Allows use of the specified CRL file even if it has expired.

template.description: Specifies the TLS template type to use for authenticating TLS servers.

keyType.title: Key Type
keyType.description:
The cryptographic algorithm family used to sign the JSON web key. The valid values are:

* **rsa** - Uses RSA algorithms.
* **octet** - Uses ECDSA algorithms.
* **elliptic-curve** - Uses HMAC algorithms.

algType.description: The cryptographic algorithm used to sign the JSON web key.

includeX5c.description: Determines whether the JWKS endpoint response contains a chain of one or more PKIX certificates.

useClientSecret.description: Determines whether the OAuth client uses the client secret instead of the shared secret to authenticate to the authorization server when the key type is set to octet.

publicExponent.description: The encryption exponent value for an RSA public key in base64url-encoded format.

yCoordinate.title: Y Coordinate
yCoordinate.description: The y coordinate for an elliptic curve in base64url-encoded format.

curve.title: Curve

certThumbprintSha1.description: The base64url-encoded SHA-1 thumbprint of the DER encoding of the X.509 certificate.

certThumbprintSha256.title: SHA-256 Thumbprint
certThumbprintSha256.description: The base64url-encoded SHA-256 thumbprint of the DER encoding of the X.509 certificate.

sharedSecret.title: Shared Secret
sharedSecret.description: The shared secret for the symmetric JSON web key when key type is set to octet. This value is not required if using client secret.

passphrase.title: Key Passphrase
passphrase.description: The passphrase used to encrypt the certificate key provided in the certKey property.

cert.title: Certificate
cert.description: The certificate file for RSA or Elliptic Curve key type that this JSON web key uses to verify the JSON web token. Values derived from this file are a part of the JSON web key set endpoint response.

includeX5c.description: Determines whether the JWKS endpoint response contains a chain of one or more PKIX certificates.

contentEncryptionAlgorithm.title: Content Encryption Algorithm
contentEncryptionAlgorithm.description: The data encryption algorithm used to generate the JWE token.

modulus.title: Modulus
modulus.description: The modulus value for an RSA public key in base64url-encoded format.

publicExponent.title: Public Exponent
publicExponent.description: The exponent value for an RSA public key in base64url-encoded format.

certThumbprintSha1.title: SHA-1 Thumbprint
certThumbprintSha1.description: The base64url-encoded SHA-1 thumbprint of the DER encoding of the X.509 certificate.

certThumbprintSha256.title: SHA-256 Thumbprint
certThumbprintSha256.description: The base64url-encoded SHA-256 thumbprint of the DER encoding of the X.509 certificate.

passphrase.title: Key Passphrase
passphrase.description: The passphrase used to encrypt the certificate key provided in the certKey property.

cert.title: Certificate
cert.description: The certificate file the JSON Web Key uses to verify the JSON web token. Values derived from this file are a part of the JSON web key set endpoint response.

certKey.title: Certificate Key
certKey.description: The certificate key this JSON web key uses to sign the JSON web token.

certChain.title: Certificate Chain
certChain.description: The certificate chain this JSON web key uses to validate the certificate. Values derived from this chain are a part of the JSON web key set endpoint response.

issuer.description: Specifies the URL for the issuer of the JSON web token.

audience.description: Specifies the audience for the token.

useJwtProviderListSettings.description: Specifies whether the settings configured in the jwt-provider-list of which this JWT config is a part should be used.

accessTokenExpiresIn.description: Specifies the number of minutes the access token should live.

allowedSigningAlgorithms.description: Specifies the list of allowed signing algorithms for the token.

blockedSigningAlgorithms.description: Specifies the list of blocked signing algorithms for the token.

name.description: OAuth denylist token list name.

values.description: Specifies a key-value-list that can be used to add tokens to a denylist based on the key and the list of values for that key.

name.description: OAuth provider name.

introspect.description: Specifies whether an access token can be returned.

ignoreExpiredCert.description: Specifies whether the expired AS certificate enforcement is to be ignored.

allowSelfSignedJwkCert.description: Specifies whether creating JWK config containing self-signed certificate is allowed.

trustedCaBundle.description: Specifies the trusted CA bundle for AS certificate that is used for autodiscovery of JSON web tokens and JSON web keys.

maxResponseSize.description: Size limit for all responses from all endpoints of the provider.

maxJsonNestingLayers.description: Maximum nesting layers of JSON responses that will be processed and stored.

authRealm.description: Specifies a Kerberos auth realm name (administrative name), such as TESTBED.LAB.COMPANYNET.COM.

keytabFileContent.description: Keytab file content that contains Kerberos encrypted keys; (these are derived from the Kerberos password).

keytabFileName.description: Specifies a keytab file that contains Kerberos encrypted keys; (these are derived from the Kerberos password).

name.description: Specifies the name for the Kerberos server you are configuring.

serviceName.description: Specifies the Kerberos service name; for example, HTTP.

spn.description: Specifies the Kerberos service principal name; for example, %{session.vmware.spn}.

spnFormat.description: Specifies the service principle name (SPN) format for the Kerberos AAA server. All existing Kerberos AAA servers are host-based services by default. Use Kerberos 5 NT Principal format for VMware View clients.

name.description: Specifies the name of the RADIUS server.

nasIdentifier.description: Specifies the string to identify the NAS that originates the access request.

nasIpAddress.description: Specifies the IPv4 address as RADIUS attribute 4, NAS-IP-Address, without changing the source IP address in the IP header of the RADIUS packets.

nasIpv6Address.description: Specifies the IPv6 address as RADIUS attribute 4, NAS-IP-Address, without changing the source IP address in the IP header of the RADIUS packets.

radiusCharacterSet.description: Specifies the character encoding used for the user name and password.

retries.description: Specifies the number of times the BIG-IP Next instance tries to make a connection to the RADIUS AAA server after the first attempt fails. The default value is 3.

secret.description: Specifies the shared secret password for your RADIUS AAA server.

poolName.title: Pool Name
poolName.description: A soft reference, by name, to a pool object.

l4ServerSide.title: L4 Server Side
l4ServerSide.description: A soft reference, by name, to an L4 server-side object.

ignorePersistedWeight.title: Ignore Persisted Weight
ignorePersistedWeight.description: Specifies whether to count the weight of persisted connections on pool members when making load balancing decisions. The default value is false.

serviceType.description: Specifies the type of service you use on the RADIUS server. Service types are specific to your RADIUS implementation.

timeout.description: Specifies the number of seconds to wait for a response from the RADIUS AAA server before timing out. The default value is 5.

allowCerts.title: Allow Certificates
allowCerts.description: Determines whether to allow the addition of certificates to an OCSP request.

cafile.title: Certificate Authority File
cafile.description: The name of the file containing trusted CA certificates used to verify the signature on the OCSP response.

certidDigest.title: Cert ID Digest
certidDigest.description: The algorithm for converting the client certificate and its issuer certificate to an OCSP cert ID. The cert ID is added to the OCSP request.

chain.title: Certificate Chain
chain.description: Determines whether to construct a certificate chain in the OCSP response.

checkCerts.title: Check Certificates
checkCerts.description: Determines whether to make additional checks to verify if the signer's certificate is authorized to provide the necessary status information.

description.title: Description
description.description: The description for the OCSP responder.

explicitOcsp.title: Explicit OCSP
explicitOcsp.description:
Determines whether to explicitly trust that the OCSP signer's certificate is authorized for OCSP response signing.

**Note:** The X509 certificate has several extensions, one of which indicates whether the certificate can be used for signing. If this parameter is enabled, but the signer's certificate does not contain the OCSP signing extension field, BIG-IP Next Access does not trust the response.

ignoreAia.title: Ignore AIA
ignoreAia.description: Determines whether to ignore the URL in the certificate's AIA fields and use the URL the responder specifies.

intern.title: Intern
intern.description: Determines whether to look internally in the OCSP response for the signer's certificate.

name.title: Server Name
name.description: The responder server name.

nonce.title: Nonce
nonce.description: Determines whether the system adds a nonce extension to OCSP requests.

signDigest.title: Sign Digest
signDigest.description: The algorithm to use for certificate identification in the OCSP request.

signOther.title: Sign Other
signOther.description: Additional certificates to include in the OCSP request.

signer.title: Signer
signer.description: The certificate name to sign an OCSP request. If the signer is specified, but the signkey is not specified, then the private key is read from the same file as the certificate. The request is not signed if neither the signer nor the signkey is specified. If only the signkey is specified, then the configuration is considered invalid.

signkey.title: Signing Key
signkey.description: The signing key to sign an OCSP request.

statusAge.title: Status Age
statusAge.description: A time (in seconds) to compare to the notBefore field of a status response. Each certificate status response includes a notBefore time and an optional notAfter time. This property is used when the status response does not include the notAfter time. The value of statusAge should be more than the notBefore time.

trustOther.title: Trust Other
trustOther.description: Determines whether to trust the certificates specified in the verifyOther setting.

url.title: URL
url.description: The URL used to contact the OCSP service on the responder.

vafile.title: VA File
vafile.description: The name of the file containing explicitly-trusted responder certificates.

validityPeriod.title: Validity Period
validityPeriod.description: An acceptable error range (in seconds) for the time tolerated in an OCSP response. Each certificate status response includes a notBefore time and an optional notAfter time and the current time should fall between these two values. This parameter is used when the OCSP responder and client clocks are not synchronized, which could cause a certificate status check to fail.

verify.title: Verify
verify.description: Determines whether to verify an OCSP response signature or the nonce value.

verifyCert.title: Verify Certificate
verifyCert.description: Determines whether to verify the certificate in the OCSP response.

verifyOther.title: Verify Other
verifyOther.description: The name of the file used to search for an OCSP response signing certificate when the certificate has been omitted from the response.

verifySig.title: Verify Signature
verifySig.description: Determines whether to check the signature on the OCSP response.

dnsResolverName.title: DNS Resolver
dnsResolverName.description: The DNS resolver object to be used to perform DNS resolution.

serverTls.title: Server Side TLS Default
serverTls.description: The TLS default properties for connections with the OCSP responder.

name.title: Name
name.description: Specifies the name of the SMTP server.

encryptedConnection.title: Encrypted Connection
encryptedConnection.description: Determines whether the SMTP server requires an encrypted connection in order to send an email.

useAuthentication.title: Use Authentication
useAuthentication.description: Determines whether the SMTP server requires user validation before sending an email.

password.title: Password
password.description: Password that the SMTP server requires when validating a user.

smtpServerAddress.title: SMTP Server Host Name
smtpServerAddress.description: IP address or fully qualified domain name of the SMTP server hostname.

sourceMachineAddress.title: Local Host Name
sourceMachineAddress.description: Host name used in SMTP headers.

trustedCa.title: Trusted CA
trustedCa.description: The trusted CA bundle file to verify the SMTP server certificate when the encryptedConnection value is set to tls.

serverType.title: Server Type
serverType.description: The external server type. For SMTP Server, the value is Smtp.

username.title: Username
username.description: Username that the SMTP server requires when validating a user.

dnsResolverName.title: DNS Resolver Name
dnsResolverName.description: DNS resolver name to resolve FQDN for smtpServerAddress. Use the /L3-forwards endpoint to configure the DNS resolver.

dnsResolverName.title: DNS Resolver Name
dnsResolverName.description: The DNS resolver to be used by the server to resolve DNS names for endpoint URIs.

tls.description: The Server Side TLS that the HTTP Connector Transport uses to encrypt communication for the HTTP Connector.

maxResponseSize.title: Maximum Response Size
maxResponseSize.description: The limit for the response size, in bytes, that the HTTP Connector Request can receive. The Maximum Response Size limit is ignored if the Response Action in the associated HTTP Connector Request is set to Ignore.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: The timeout in seconds for the HTTP Connector Transport connection.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the HTTP Connector Request.

transport.description: The HTTP Connector Transport associated with this HTTP Connector Request. It defines the connection details such as the DNS server and connection limits.

url.title: URL
url.description: The URL to which HTTP Connector requests are relayed. For a subsession or perflow variable, you can specify encoding the variable as raw, URL, or JSON string. For example, %{subsession.variable.name[:encoding ]} or {perflow.variable.name[:encoding ]}.

method.title: HTTP Method
method.description: The method used to relay the request headers, body, response headers, and action.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.description:
The type of authentication used to authenticate between the HTTP Connector and the HTTP server.

When using custom authentication, username, password and token fields can be referenced as %{username}, %{password} and {token} variables in the URL, Request Header or Request Body. Additionally, %{basic_auth} can be used to insert a Basic authorization header value. The valid values are:

* **none** - Involves no authorization.
* **basic** - Uses basic authorization.
* **bearer** - Uses security token as bearer token.
* **custom** - Uses custom authorization header

username.title: Username
username.description: The username for Basic or Custom authentication.

password.title: Password
password.description: The password for Basic or Custom authentication.

token.title: Token
token.description: The token used for Bearer or Custom authentication.

requestHeaders.title: Request Headers
requestHeaders.description: The request headers to send to the HTTP server. You can specify the exact header value pairs or subsession variables. You can also encode the variable as raw, URL, or JSON string.

requestBody.title: Request Body
requestBody.description: The request body to send to the HTTP server when the method is POST or PUT. You can also specify subsession variables as placeholders that are substituted with their values when the request is sent.

responseHeaders.title: Response Headers
responseHeaders.description: The response headers that will be saved as subsession variables.

responseAction.title: Response Action
responseAction.description:
Determines how the response data is processed. The valid values are:

* **ignore** - The response will not be saved.
* **parse** - The response will be parsed. Select this value only if the response is in JSON format.
* **save** - The response will be saved in a single subsession variable.

leasePoolMember.description: IPv4 or IPv6 Lease pool member.

startAddr.description: An IPv4 or IPv6 address with no prefix specified.

endAddr.description: An IPv4 or IPv6 address with no prefix specified.

This box appears only if you selected Network Access or Full as the type of webtop. With a network access webtop, the webtop automatically minimizes to the tray. With a full webtop, the webtop minimizes to the system tray only after the network access connection is started.
minimizeToTray.title: Minimize To Tray

displayOrder.description: Specifies the display order of this webtop section relative to others on a webtop. Setting this value to 0 places this webtop section first.

initialState.description: Specifies the initial display state of this webtop section. Set this value to expanded to display all resources or collapsed to display only the webtop section name.

sort.description: Specifies the display order for resources. Set this value alphabetically to sort the resources by name. Set this value by-priority to sort them in the priority order. Each resource is prioritized based on its corresponding order in the webtopSectionResources array.

applicationUri.description: Specifies the URI for the webtop link to be displayed on the webtop.

webtopSectionResources.description: Specifies an array of resources to display in a webtop section. Each resource is prioritized based on its corresponding order in the webtopSectionResources array.

useLocalProxy.description: Determines whether the browser uses the proxy configured on the client before establishing a network access connection.

exclusionList.title: Proxy Exclusion list
exclusionList.description:
The Web addresses that do not need to be accessed through your proxy server. Wild cards can be used to match domain and host names or addresses.

For example, www.*.com, 128.*, 240.8, 8., mygroup.*, *.* , and so forth.

codecInfoT.title: Compression Codec Info

compressionAdaptive.title: Adaptive Compression Option
compressionAdaptive.description: Determines whether to enable adaptive compression. Adaptive compression automatically selects a compression type based on network and traffic characteristics.

compressionDeflate.title: Deflate Compression Option
compressionDeflate.description: Determines whether to enable Deflate compression. Deflate compression uses the least CPU resources but compresses the least effectively.

compressionBzip2.title: BZIP2 Compression Option
compressionBzip2.description: Determines whether to enable Bzip2 compression. Bzip2 compression uses the most CPU resources but compresses effectively.

compressionLzo.title: LZO Compression Option
compressionLzo.description: Determines whether to enable LZO compression. LZO compression offers a balance between CPU resources and compression ratio, compressing more than deflate but with fewer CPU resources than Bzip2.

networkAccessOptimizedApplicationT.description: Optimized application tunnels. It is set of compression characteristics that are applied to traffic flowing from the network access client to a specific IP address, network, or host, on a specified port or range of ports. The application destination can be a host (FQDN), IP address (IPv4 or IPv6), or a network. When optimizing a network, leave the destination host value empty and specify the IP address and subnet mask. An optimized tunnel provides a TCP Layer 4 connection to an application. It take precedence over standard network access tunnels.

name.title: Name
name.description: The optimized application name.

host.title: Host Name
host.description: A fully qualified domain name (FQDN) for the application destination. This value should be empty when setting optimization to a network.

ip.description: An IPv4 or IPv6 address with no prefix specified.

portRange.description: The port range on which to optimize traffic.

codecInfo.description: Defines compression codec related parameters to configure for an optimized application.

name.title: Network Access Resource Name
name.description: Network Access Resource Name.

description.title: Resource Description
description.description: An optional description for the network access resource.

supportedIpVersion.title: IP Protocol Version
supportedIpVersion.description: Sets the Network Access tunnel to support either an IPv4 or IPv6 lease pool or both IPv4 and IPv6 lease pools.

leasePoolName.title: IPv4 Lease Pool Name
leasePoolName.description: The IPv4 lease pool to assign leased IPv4 addresses to remote network access clients.

ipv6LeasePoolName.title: IPv6 Lease Pool Name
ipv6LeasePoolName.description: The IPv6 lease pool to assign leased IPv6 addresses to remote network access clients.

autolaunch.title: Auto Launch
autolaunch.description: Determines whether to automatically start the network access resource when the user reaches a full webtop. When assigning network access resources to a full webtop, only one network access resource can have auto launch enabled.

networkTunnelEnabled.title: Enable Network Tunnel
networkTunnelEnabled.description: Determines whether to configure a network access tunnel for providing network access.

snatType.description: Specifies the SNAT pool type. Default is AUTOMAP.

snatPoolName.title: SNAT Pool Name
snatPoolName.description: The name of a SNAT pool.

splitTunneling.title: Split Tunnelling
splitTunneling.description: Determines whether only the traffic targeted to a specified address space is sent over the network access tunnel and all other traffic bypasses the tunnel.

proxyArp.title: Proxy ARP
proxyArp.description:
Determines whether to enable proxy ARP.

When you implement Proxy ARP for a network access resource, remote VPN tunnel clients can use IP addresses from the LAN IP subnet without additional network infrastructure changes.

Ranges of IP addresses from the LAN subnet can be configured in the lease pools and assigned to tunnel clients. When a host on the LAN sends traffic to a tunnel client, an ARP query is sent to request the client address. BIG-IP Next Access then responds with its own MAC address.

Traffic is then sent to network access, and forwarded to the client over the network access tunnel.

compression.description: Compression method. Either None or GZIP.

addressSpaceDhcpRequestsExcluded.title: Exclude DHCP Requests
addressSpaceDhcpRequestsExcluded.description: Determines whether requests from IP addresses using DHCP are excluded from accessing the network.

addressSpaceIncludeDnsNames.title: Address Space Include Dns Names
addressSpaceIncludeDnsNames.description: Provides a list of domain names describing the target LAN DNS addresses. You must specify a DNS address space with split tunneling. If you do not specify a DNS address space, or *, DNS does not work over split tunnels on Windows, macOS, Linux, or iOS.

addressSpaceExcludeDnsNames.title: Address Space Exclude Dns Names
addressSpaceExcludeDnsNames.description: Specifies DNS address spaces for which traffic is not forced through the tunnel.

addressSpaceSubnetsInclude.title: Address Space Subnets Include
addressSpaceSubnetsInclude.description: Specifies a list of endpoint IP addresses or network addresses in a CIDR format. When using split tunneling, only the traffic to these addresses and network segments goes through the tunnel configured for Network Access. You can add multiple address spaces to the list, one at a time.

addressSpaceSubnetsExclude.title: Address Space Subnets Exclude
addressSpaceSubnetsExclude.description: Specifies address spaces for which traffic is not forced through the tunnel. For each address space that you want to exclude, provide the IP address or network address in the CIDR format.

addressSpaceLocalDnsServersExcluded.title: Exclude Local DNS Servers
addressSpaceLocalDnsServersExcluded.description: Determines whether to exclude local DNS servers configured on the client before establishing a network access connection.

addressSpaceLocalSubnetsExcluded.title: Exclude Local Subnet Address spaces
addressSpaceLocalSubnetsExcluded.description:
Determines whether to exclude local access to any host or subnet in routes configured in the client routing table.

When set to true, integrated IP filtering is not supported.

addressSpaceProtect.title: Protect IP address spaces
addressSpaceProtect.description:
Determines whether to protect the IP address spaces whose traffic is forced through the tunnel.

Prohibits routing table changes during Network Access connection.

warnBeforeApplicationLaunch.description: Determines whether to warn the user that an application is being launched.

clientInterfaceSpeed.title: Client Interface Speed
clientInterfaceSpeed.description: The maximum speed of the client interface connection, in bits per second.

clientIpFilterEngine.title: IP Filtering Engine
clientIpFilterEngine.description: Determines whether to protect the resource from outside traffic (traffic generated by network devices on the client's LAN) and ensure it is not leaking traffic to the client's LAN.

clientPowerManagement.description: Specifies how to interact with Windows power management features.

clientProxy.title: Enable Client Proxy
clientProxy.description: Determines whether this resource handles a client proxy.

clientProxyConfig.title: Client Proxy Configuration
clientProxyConfig.description:
Additional settings for client proxy connections for this network resource.

Client proxy settings apply to the proxy behind the BIG-IP Next Access and do not affect the VPN tunnel transport or interact with the TLS or DTLS configuration.

Use client proxy settings when intranet web servers are not directly accessible from the internal subnet.

Client proxy settings apply only to HTTP, HTTPS, and FTP connections. SOCKS connections can also be proxied with a custom PAC file.

clientTrayIcon.title: Client Tray Icon
clientTrayIcon.description: Determines whether to display the client tray icon.

dnsHostConfig.description: Holds DNS and Static host configurations.

staticHosts.description: An array of static hosts IP and hostname tuples.

driveMappings.title: Drive Mappings
driveMappings.description: An Array of configured mapped network drives.

dtls.title: DTLS
dtls.description: Determines whether to use Datagram Transport Level Security for the network access connection.

dtlsPort.title: DTLS Port
dtlsPort.description: The DTLS port number that the resource uses for secure UDP traffic.

idleTimeoutThreshold.title: Session Update Threshold (in bytes)
idleTimeoutThreshold.description: The average byte rate that either ingress or egress tunnel traffic must exceed for the tunnel to update a session. If the average byte rate falls below the specified threshold, the system applies the inactivity timeout, as defined in the Access profile, to the session.

idleTimeoutWindow.title: Session Update Window (in seconds)
idleTimeoutWindow.description: The time value in seconds used to calculate the EMA (Exponential Moving Average) byte rate of ingress and egress tunnel traffic.

microsoftNetworkClient.title: Client for Microsoft Networks
microsoftNetworkClient.description: Determines whether to allow the client PC to access remote resources over a VPN connection.

microsoftNetworkServer.title: File and Printer Sharing Server for Microsoft Networks
microsoftNetworkServer.description: Determines whether to allow remote hosts to access shared resources on the client computer over the network access connection. This allows the VPN to work in reverse and a VPN user to share files and printers with remote LAN users and other VPN users.

preserveSourcePortStrict.description: Specifies that the system preserves the value configured for the source port.

provideClientCert.title: Provide client Certificate
provideClientCert.description:
Determines whether client certificates are required to establish an SSL connection.

* Set to **true**, if client certificates are required to establish an SSL connection.
* Set to **false**, if client certificates are not required to establish an SSL connection. However, the client certificates can be still requested within an SSL connection as part of higher layer protocol establishments.

syncWithActiveDirectory.title: Reconnect to Domain - Sync with Active Directory
syncWithActiveDirectory.description:
Determines whether to synchronize with Active Directory policies on connection establishment. When set to true, the network access connection emulates the Windows logon process for a client on an Active Directory domain. Network policies are synchronized when the connection is established or at logoff.

The following items are synchronized:

* Logon scripts are started as specified in the user profile.
* Drives are mapped as specified in the user profile.
* Group policies are synchronized as specified in the user profile. Group Policy logon scripts are started when the connection is established, and Group Policy logoff scripts are run when the network access connection is stopped.

executeLogoffScripts.title: Reconnect to Domain - Run Logoff Scripts
executeLogoffScripts.description:
Determines whether to run logoff scripts on connection termination. 

* When set to **true**, run logoff scripts as configured on the Active Directory domain when the connection is terminated.
* When set to **false**, do nothing.

formIdentification.title: Form Identification
formIdentification.description:
Determines how to find the HTML logon form in the HTML body of the logon page. The form identification methods are action, id, name, form parameters, or form order.

Set the type parameter to:
* **action-attribute** - To identify the form using the form action value.
* **id-attribute** - To identify the form using the form ID value.
* **name-attribute** - To identify the form using the form name value.
* **form-parameters** - To identify the form using the general form parameters.
* **form-order** - To identify the form using the relative order of the form on the logon page.

submitDetection.title: Submit Detection
submitDetection.description:
Determines how to detect a successful form submit. The form submit detection methods are auto submit, manual submit using URI, manual submit using cookie, or manual submit using header.

Set the type parameter to:
* **auto** - To automatically detect a successful form submit.
* **uri** - To use the URI scheme to detect submit.
* **cookie** - To use the cookie name and value to detect submit.
* **header** - To use the header name and value to detect submit.

formRequestDetection.title: Form Request Detection
formRequestDetection.description:
The form request detection parameters.

Set the type parameter to:
* **header** - To use the header name and value to detect form request.
* **cookie** - To use the cookie name and value to detect form request.
* **URI** - To use one or more URIs to detect form request.

javascriptInjection.title: Javascript Injection
javascriptInjection.description:
Settings to change the automatically generated JavaScript code that gets inserted into the logon page. The Javascript injection methods are auto, custom, or extra.

Set the type parameter to:
* **auto** - To use the default JavaScript that BIG-IP Next creates.
* **custom** - To use only the code that you enter in the submitJavascript parameter.
* **extra** - To use the JavaScript that BIG-IP Next automatically generates appended by any code that you enter in the submitJavascript parameter.

loginDetection.title: Login Detection
loginDetection.description:
Determines how to detect a successful logon. The login detection methods are none, cookie or url.

Set the type parameter to:
* **cookie** - To check for successful logon based on the value you specify in the CookieValue field.
* **redirect-uri** - To check for successful logon based on the URI value you specify in the requestValue field.
* **none** - To not check for successful logon.

userIdentityMethod.title: User Identification Method
userIdentityMethod.description:
Determines how to identify users. The valid values are:

* **http** - Identifies clients using HTTP.
* **ip-address** - Identifies clients by IP address. When this value is set, both NTLM Auth Configuration and Kerberos Auth Configuration are optional. Use this option only in an environment where a client IP address is unique and can be trusted, typically, in an internal network. This option is not for use with internal NAT or shared systems. Selecting this option ignores the cookies.
* **credentials** - Identifies clients using the NTLM Auth or Kerberos Auth or both. When this option is selected, you must choose one authentication type.
* **oauth-token** - Identifies clients using OAuth token.

logoutUriTimeout.description: Specifies the timeout used to delay logout for the customized logout URIs defined in the logout URI Include list.

expression.description:
The Tcl expression to choose a branch. The Tcl expression can contain one or more expressions (expr) or return commands.

An **expr** evaluates an expression and returns the result. A **return** simply returns the result and can be used to set the variable to a numeric value or string. When evaluating a branch rule, Access obtains session variables from system memory using the Tcl command **mcget**.

For example, expr {[mcget {perflow.branching.url}] contains "domain.com"}.

confirm-box.title: Customization For Confirm Box
confirm-box.description: Customizable strings and settings for the confirm box in all supported languages.

items.description: Customized strings for each language.

language.title: Language
language.description: Language of customized strings. The ISO 639-1 language codes should be used.

strings.description: Customizable strings and settings for the confirm box.

option1Image.title: Field 1 image
option1Image.description: The image that appears in the first option field on the confirm box.

continueMessage.title: Continue
continueMessage.description: The text for the continue message.

option2Image.title: Field 2 image
option2Image.description: The image that appears in the second option field on the confirm box.

cancelMessage.title: Cancel
cancelMessage.description: The text for the cancel message.

expression.description:
The Tcl expression to choose a branch. The Tcl expression can contain one or more expressions (expr) or return commands.

An **expr** evaluates an expression and returns the result. A **return** simply returns the result and can be used to set the variable to a numeric value or string. When evaluating a branch rule, Access obtains session variables from system memory using the Tcl command **mcget**.

For example, expr {[mcget {perflow.decision_box.result}] == 1}.

categoryLookupType.title: Category Lookup Type
categoryLookupType.description:
The category types in which to search for the requested URL. The valid values are:

* **all** - Always process full list of both custom and standard categories.
* **custom-first** - Process custom categories first and continue to standard categories if not found.
* **custom-only** - Process custom categories only.
* **standard** - Process standard categories only.

resetOnFailure.title: Reset On Failure
resetOnFailure.description: Determines whether to enable or disable reset on server failure. When set to enable, SWG send a TCP reset to the client on a server failure.

safesearchMode.title: Safe Search Mode
safesearchMode.description: Determines whether to enable or disable safe search for supported search engines. This feature prevents offensive content and images from showing up in search results.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the category lookup item.

categorizationInput.title: Categorization Input
categorizationInput.description:
Determines where to obtain the URL that is being requested. The valid values are:

* **http-connect-host** - For connections that are passing through an upstream proxy, this option uses information from the HTTP Connect header and matches only the hostname. The Category Lookup item functions only on the transparent HTTP virtual servers and fails if the policy is attached to explicit HTTP virtual servers.
* **http-uri** - For HTTP traffic, this option specifies performing a URL-based lookup. This cannot be used for SSL Bypass decisions.
* **sni** - For HTTPS traffic, this option specifies performing a host-based lookup.
* **subject-cn** - For HTTPS traffic, this option specifies performing a host-based lookup.

perRequestPolicyMacro.description: Access policy macro used per request.

maxMacroLoopCount.title: Maximum Macro Loop Count
maxMacroLoopCount.description: The loop count for subroutine policy. The end-user gets the specified number of authentication retries if there is an authentication action in the subroutine.

inactivityTimeout.title: Inactivity Timeout
inactivityTimeout.description: The number of seconds of inactivity after subroutine validation that the subsession is invalidated due to inactivity.

maxSubsessionLifetime.title: Maximum Subsession Life
maxSubsessionLifetime.description: The number of seconds after validation when the subsession is considered expired.

subroutineTimeout.title: Subsession Timeout
subroutineTimeout.description: The number of seconds that the user has to complete the subroutine. A subroutine is interactive; the user must acknowledge all requests (such as typing credentials or making a choice) within this time.

gatingCriteria.title: Gating Criteria
gatingCriteria.description:
A perflow variable (to represent a particular resource or group of resources) or remains blank (to represent all resources).

BIG-IP Next Access uses this value to determine whether to run the subroutine. If a subsession already exists for the particular resource (such as a URL or an application) or the group of resources (such as those represented by a URL category or an application family), Access does not run the subroutine again for the current request, but uses the same subsession, applying the result that was obtained when the subroutine ran.

* If Access does not find a matching subsession, it validates the gating criteria before running the subroutine.
* If the field contains a perflow variable, Access looks up its value.
* If the perflow variable does not contain a value, Access returns an error.
* If the perflow variable contains a valid value or if the gating criteria is blank, Access runs the subroutine.

savePasswordMethodT.title: Save Password Method
savePasswordMethodT.description:
The method to save passwords for password caching. User passwords can be saved for reconnection purposes for a specified period.

* **disk** - The user password is encrypted and saved on the device with no expiration.
* **memory** - The user password is cached in the application on the user's device for a configurable period. Use the Password Cache Expiration field to configure the number of minutes you want the password to be cached in memory.
* **none** - The password is not cached.

ecComponentUpdateT.title: Access Client Component Update
ecComponentUpdateT.description:
Determines whether to automatically update Access Client packages when newer versions are available. The valid values are,

* **no** - The Access Client does not automatically attempt an upgrade when connecting to BIG-IP Next Access.
* **prompt** - The Access Client prompts users to upgrade the components when connecting to BIG-IP Next Access.
* **yes** - The Access Client automatically attempts an upgrade when connecting to BIG-IP Next Access.

serverEntry.title: Server List
serverEntry.description: The list of servers to provide as connection options when the client connects.

alias.title: Alias
alias.description: The alias name for the server.

host.title: Host Name
host.description: The host name for the server.

androidDeviceLockMethodT.title: Android Device Lock Method
androidDeviceLockMethodT.description:
The type of password that the user must enter to unlock the device. The valid values are:

* **alphabetic** - The password must contain only alphabets.
* **alphanumeric** - The password must contain a combination of alphabets and numbers.
* **any** - The password can include alphabets and numbers without restriction.
* **numeric** - The password must contain a numeric PIN.

androidEcDeviceLockComplexityT.title: Android EC Device Lock Complexity
androidEcDeviceLockComplexityT.description:
The device lock complexity that the client should enforce on the device. Refer to [Android's Password Complexity](https://developer.android.com/work/versions/android-10#screen_lock_quality_check) for details on the minimum password requirements for each category.

The following password rules are applicable for the different device lock complexity catagories

* **high** - The password must meet one of the following rules
  * **alphabetic** - Minimum length of six characters.
  * **alphanumeric** - Minimum length of six characters.
  * **numeric** - Minimum length of eight characters without repetition or ordered sequences.
* **medium** - The password must meet one of the following rules
  * **alphabetic** - Minimum length of four characters.
  * **alphanumeric** - Minimum length of four characters.
  * **numeric** - Minimum length of four characters without repetition or ordered sequences.
* **low** - The password can be a pattern or a PIN with repeating numbers or ordered sequences.
* **none** - The password is not required.

logonMethodT.title: Enforce Logon Method
logonMethodT.description:
The logon method that the app is forced to use. This enforced logon method cannot be changed by the user.

* **native** - Access Client uses the native logon method.
* **web** - Access Client uses the web logon method.
* **none** - Logon method is not enforced.

name.title: Client Policy Name
name.description: The client policy name.

ecSaveServersOnExit.title: Access Client Save Server List on Exit
ecSaveServersOnExit.description: Determines whether to maintain a list of recently used BIG-IP Next Access and user-entered servers. Access Client lists the servers defined in the connectivity policy and sorts them by most recent access, whether this option is selected or not. However, it lists the user-entered servers only if this option is selected.

ecReuseWinlogonSession.title: Access Client Reuse Windows Logon Session
ecReuseWinlogonSession.description: Determines whether to reuse a Windows logon session for a BIG-IP Next Access session.

ecReuseWinlogonCreds.title: Access Client Reuse Windows Logon Credentials
ecReuseWinlogonCreds.description:
Determines whether to reuse Windows logon credentials to start a BIG-IP Next Access session.

To support this option, you must also include the User Logon Credentials Access Service in the Windows client package for this connectivity profile and you must ensure that the access policy includes an uncustomized Logon Page action.

ecRunLogoffScript.title: Access Client Run Session Logoff Script
ecRunLogoffScript.description: Determines whether to run an administrator-defined logoff script after users disconnect from Network Access VPN. The administrator specifies parameters which are passed by Access Client to the script file. These parameters are defined by the session variable session.edgeclient.scripting.logoff.params. The client retrieves parameters from BIG-IP Next after session establishment. The administrator has the flexibility to set up variable values according to policy branching. Each time the Access Client closes an Access session, the configured script is invoked. On Windows, the script is located at "C:\Program Files\F5 VPN\scripts\onSessionTermination.bat".

ecWarnBeforeScriptLaunch.title: Access Client Show Warning Before Launching Script
ecWarnBeforeScriptLaunch.description: Determines whether to display a warning before launching the pre-defined script on session termination.

ecSavePasswordTimeout.title: Access Client Password Cache Expiration (minutes)
ecSavePasswordTimeout.description: The number of minutes to save the password in memory for the Windows/Mac Access Client.

ecLocationDnsList.title: Access Client Location DNS List
ecLocationDnsList.description: The list of DNS suffixes. It enables the client to automatically connect when a client is not on the local network (not on the list) and automatically disconnect when the client is on the local network.

serverList.title: SSL VPN Server List
serverList.description: The list of virtual servers for the connectivity access policy.

androidEcRequireDeviceAuth.title: Android Access Client Require Device Authentication
androidEcRequireDeviceAuth.description: Determines whether Android Access Client requires device authentication.

androidEcSavePasswordMethod.title: Android Access Client Save Password Method

androidEcSavePasswordTimeout.title: Android Access Client Password Cache Expiration (minutes)
androidEcSavePasswordTimeout.description: The number of minutes to save the password in memory for the Android Access Client.

androidEcEnforceDeviceLock.title: Android Access Client Enforce Device Lock
androidEcEnforceDeviceLock.description:
Determines whether to configure device locking methods to enhance security on the Android Access Client.

Access Client does not support pattern locking.

androidEcMinPasscodeLength.title: Android Access Client Minimum Passcode Length

androidEcMaxInactivityTime.title: Android Access Client Maximum Inactivity Time
androidEcMaxInactivityTime.description: The maximum tunnel inactivity time in minutes.

iosEcRequireDeviceAuth.description: Determines whether iOS Access Client requires device authentication.

iosEcSavePasswordTimeout.description: The number of minutes to save the password in memory for the iOS Access Client.

iosEcOnDemandDisconnectTimeout.title: iOS Access Client On Demand Disconnect Timeout
iosEcOnDemandDisconnectTimeout.description: The number of minutes before VPN on demand times out for iOS Access Client.

macosEcSavePasswordTimeout.description: The number of minutes to save the password in memory for macOS Access Client.

chromeosEcSavePasswordTimeout.description: The number of minutes to save the password in memory for the ChromeOS Access Client.

description.title: Description
description.description: The user defined description for PPP profile.

lcpEchoFailure.title: LCP Echo Failure
lcpEchoFailure.description: The number of consecutive PPP LCP echo messages that must go unanswered for the server to drop PPP connection. For example, if the server sends number of consecutive PPP LCP Echo Request messages that go unanswered (by Echo Reply), it will close the PPP connection.

lcpEchoInterval.title: LCP Echo Interval
lcpEchoInterval.description: The interval, in seconds, between the PPP LCP Echo Request messages that the server sends to the peer (client).

vj.title: Van Jacobson Compression
vj.description: Determines whether the system uses Van Jacobson Header Compression (also known as VJ compression, or just Header Compression). It is a data compression protocol described in RFC 1144, specifically designed by Van Jacobson to improve TCP/IP performance over slow serial links.

autoLasthop.title: Auto Lasthop
autoLasthop.description: Determines whether to enable or disable tracking the source MAC address of incoming connections and the return traffic from pools to the source MAC address, regardless of the routing table. When enabled, BIG-IP Next sends return traffic from pools to the MAC address that transmitted the request, even if the routing table points to a different network or interface.

description.title: Description
description.description: The user defined description for the PPP tunnel.

mtu.title: Maximum Transmission Unit
mtu.description: The maximum length allowable for a layer 2 packet on an Ethernet network.

tos.title: Type of Service Value
tos.description: Returns (or sets) the Type of Service (TOS) value encoded within a packet.

usePmtu.title: Use PMTU
usePmtu.description:
Determines whether the tunnel uses PMTU (Path MTU).

* If enabled and the tunnel MTU is set to 0, the tunnel will use the PMTU information.
* If enabled and the tunnel MTU is fixed to a non-zero value, the tunnel will use the minimum of PMTU and MTU.
* If disabled, the tunnel will use fixed MTU or calculate its MTU using tunnel encapsulation configurations.

name.title: Name
name.description: The connectivity policy name.

policyType.title: Policy Type
policyType.description:
The type of policy.

* For a connectivity access policy, this value is ConnectivityAccessPolicy.

citrixClientBundle.title: Citrix Client Bundle
citrixClientBundle.description: The Citrix client bundle to download on the client. It enables the delivery of a Citrix Receiver client to a user's Windows computer when a client is not currently installed or when a newer client is available. By default, a connectivity profile includes the default Citrix bundle, default-citrix-client-bundle, which contains a download URL, receiver.citrix.com.

compressBufferSize.title: Compress Buffer Size
compressBufferSize.description: The number of bytes for the size of the output buffers containing compressed data. You can increase the compression buffer size by increments of 1024 bytes.

compressGzipLevel.title: Compress Gzip Level
compressGzipLevel.description:
The level of compression that is applied to the connection.

* A **lower** number provides a lower, but faster compression ratio.
* A *higher** number provides a higher compression ratio; however, it requires more processing, which makes it slower.
* The **default** compression level is **6** - Optimal Compression (Recommended), which provides a balance between level of compression and CPU processing time.

compressGzipMemlevel.title: Compress Gzip Mem Level
compressGzipMemlevel.description:
The amount of memory that the compression library (zlib) uses for its internal compression buffers. Specify a value between 1 and 256 kilobytes. Multiples of **N x 1024**.

The **default** (8 kilobytes) is the optimal setting for the most common cases.

However, sites that primarily serve larger files may benefit from increasing this value. You must take into consideration the average size of the files the system is compressing when you change this setting.

compressGzipWindowsize.title: Compress Gzip Windwos Size
compressGzipWindowsize.description:
The amount of memory that the system uses for the compression history buffer. Specify a value between 1 and 128 kilobytes. Multiples of **N x 1024**.

The **default** (16 kilobytes) is the optimal setting for the most common cases.

This value is similar to gzip Memory Level, but zlib uses the value differently. Like the gzip Memory Level setting, sites that primarily serve larger files may benefit from increasing this value. You must take into consideration the average size of the files that are being compressed when you change this setting.

compressCpusaver.title: Compress CPU Saver
compressCpusaver.description: Determines whether to monitor the percentage of CPU usage and adjusts compression rates automatically when the CPU usage reaches either the high value or the low value.

compressCpusaverHigh.title: Compress CPU Saver High
compressCpusaverHigh.description: The percentage of CPU usage at which the system starts automatically decreasing the amount of content being compressed, as well as the amount of applied compression.

compressCpusaverLow.title: Compress CPU Saver Low
compressCpusaverLow.description: The percentage of CPU usage at which the system resumes content compression at the user-defined rates.

compression.title: Compression
compression.description: Determines whether to enable compression. The server compares the available compression types configured here, with the available compression types on the client, and chooses the most effective mutual compression setting.

compressionAdaptive.title: Adaptive Compression
compressionAdaptive.description: Determines whether to enable adaptive compression between the client and the server.

compressionDeflateLevel.title: Compression Deflate Level
compressionDeflateLevel.description: The compression level for deflate compression. A higher number provides a higher compression ratio; however, it requires more processing, which makes it slower.

compressionCodecs.title: Compression Codecs
compressionCodecs.description:
The available codecs for compression. The valid values are:

* **bzip2** - Bzip2 compression uses the most CPU resources, but compresses the most effectively.
* **deflate** - Deflate compression uses the least CPU resources, but compresses the least effectively.
* **lzo** - LZO compression offers a balance between CPU resources and compression ratio, compressing more than Deflate compression, but with less CPU resources than Bzip2.

compressIngress.title: Compress Ingress
compressIngress.description: Determines whether the ingress compression is enabled.

defaultName.title: Default Name
defaultName.description: The default name for the connectivity policy.

description.description: User defined description for the connectivity policy.

isPolicySyncCandidate.description: Determines whether the policy can be reused in the sync-only device group.

pageParam1.description: The number of the page to return. Ignored if limit parameter is absent.

sortParam1.name: sort
sortParam1.description: The column name to sort results. Prefix column name with minus sign to sort in descending order.

selectParam1.name: select
selectParam1.description: The names of the specific fields to return.

filterParam1.name: filter
filterParam1.description: Filter conditions for result queries. Queries by equality, string, or numerical conditions are possible.
